title: rustc compile-error classifier
prompt:
task_id=T4
A single rustc error block is waiting for classification. Decide which category it belongs to, chosen from EXACTLY this set:

- `move_error`         ‚Äî moved-value errors (e.g. E0382)
- `borrow_conflict`    ‚Äî simultaneous borrow-check violations (e.g. E0502, E0499)
- `trait_bound`        ‚Äî missing trait implementation (e.g. E0277)
- `lifetime`           ‚Äî reference outlives its data (e.g. E0597, E0621)
- `import_missing`     ‚Äî unresolved name / import / module (e.g. E0432, E0433)
- `type_mismatch`      ‚Äî mismatched types (e.g. E0308)

Available tools:
- error_text()          ‚Äî the full multi-line error block as rustc printed it.
- error_code_line()     ‚Äî ONLY the first line of the error (the banner like `error[E0382]: use of moved value`).
- source_snippet()      ‚Äî the underlined source lines rustc included in the block.
- search_rustc_docs(code) ‚Äî expand the official explanation for an error code.
- similar_errors(text)  ‚Äî look up one-line titles of similar past errors.

You do NOT need all of them. Call recall_knowledge(task_id="T4") first; if a prior run learned a procedure, use it. Otherwise probe what you need.

Answer via `answer(content="<category>")` with ONLY the category token ‚Äî no quotes, no extra prose.

error.full_text:
error[E0382]: borrow of moved value: `config`
  --> src/main.rs:14:18
   |
12 |     let config = load_config(&path)?;
   |         ------ move occurs because `config` has type `Config`, which does not implement the `Copy` trait
13 |     apply_config(config);
   |                  ------ value moved here
14 |     println!("{}", config.name);
   |                    ^^^^^^^^^^^^ value borrowed here after move
   |
help: consider cloning the value if the performance cost is acceptable
   |
13 |     apply_config(config.clone());
   |                  ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
similar_titles: E0382: use of moved value `buf` in for-loop body
E0382: value used after partial move
